name: kimi-tachi
description: Kimi-tachi multi-agent orchestration plugin - run workflows and manage sessions

tools.name: workflow
tools.description: Run a multi-agent workflow for a task. Auto-detects team and supports category-based parallel orchestration.

workflow_type.description: Type of workflow to run (auto, or a pattern defined by the current team)

team.description: Team ID to use (defaults to current team)

tools.name: list_agents
tools.description: List all available agents for the current (or specified) team with their roles and icons

team.description: Team ID to list agents for (defaults to current team)

tools.name: get_agent_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific agent

agent.description: Agent name to get info for

team.description: Team ID the agent belongs to (defaults to current team)

tools.name: check_compatibility
tools.description: Check kimi-cli version compatibility with kimi-tachi

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: List subagents and background tasks across recent sessions (filesystem scan)

session_id.description: Optional session ID to filter to a single session

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Get detailed status for a specific session including subagents and file sizes

session_id.description: Session ID to get status for

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Search code memory for relevant information (project-level search)

tools.name: memory_global_search
tools.description: Search across all registered projects (cross-project knowledge)

query.description: Search query for cross-project knowledge (e.g., 'JWT implementation', 'database pattern')

tools.name: memory_recall_agent
tools.description: Recall context and memories for a specific agent before starting work

task.description: Current task description to find relevant memories

tools.name: memory_store_decision
tools.description: Store an important decision or finding to memory

content.description: Decision or finding to store (e.g., 'Use Redis for session storage', 'API rate limit: 100 req/min')
